sourceMovie.title: Jason Lives - Friday the 13th Part VI
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Jason Lives - Friday the 13th Part VI for the same reason it broke through in 1986: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a thriller rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Tom McLoughlin keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The horror and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Halloween
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Jason Lives - Friday the 13th Part VI clicked for you, Halloween (1978) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Jason Lives - Friday the 13th Part VI?
faq.answer: Try Halloween, Halloween, Scream VI—each shares DNA with Jason Lives - Friday the 13th Part VI while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Jason Lives - Friday the 13th Part VI?
faq.answer: Jason Lives - Friday the 13th Part VI (1986) is best described as Horror, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Jason Lives - Friday the 13th Part VI part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Friday the 13th Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
